summary: Persistent privileged service with IM-driven remote command execution and unpinned auto-self-update
details: On install, scripts/post-install.js auto-registers the package as a privileged system service: on Windows administrator shells it uses node-windows to install `claw-subagent-service` as a SYSTEM service, sets `sc.exe failure... restart/0/restart/0/restart/0` and `start= auto`, and starts it without further user interaction. The service exposes three installer-relevant harms: (1) Backdoor: the worker connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM channel (appKey `bmdehs6pbyyks`) using a token fetched from `newsradar.dreamdt.cn`; inbound messages of type COMMAND/DEVICE_CONTROL/CHAT_MESSAGE are routed to `executeCommand`, which spawns shell via `spawn(command, { shell: true, cwd: os.tmpdir() })` in service/rongcloud/openclaw-client.js and runs `openclaw agent -m <attacker-supplied message>` and start/stop/restart/uninstall actions — anyone with control of the publisher's RongCloud account can execute shell commands on every installed host as SYSTEM/root. (2) Unpinned self-update as SYSTEM: service/updater.js polls `npm view claw-subagent-service version --json` every 6 hours and runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<version>` followed by a worker restart, so any future version the publisher pushes to npm is silently fetched and executed with system privileges. (3) Telemetry exfiltration: HeartbeatManager and DashboardReporter automatically collect MAC address, CPU/memory/uptime/platform/arch, and read `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl` (assistant message content, model usage, token counts, project/task lists) and ship them over the RongCloud channel to `newsradar.dreamdt.cn` with no opt-out. The combination — auto-installed privileged service + persistent IM C2 + unpinned root-level auto-update — is a remote-control surface over every installer's host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
